items.title: Deadline for Gaza ceasefire phase one nears -- can phase two be implemented? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The first six-week phase of the Gaza ceasefire deal is expiring. Hamas has released dozens of hostages in exchange for Israel setting free hundreds of Palestinian prisoners. But it's not clear whether negotiators meeting in Cairo can agree on the terms for stage two.

0:00 Gaza ceasefire report

items.title: Turkey: PKK militants declare ceasefire | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Kurdish PKK militants have declared a ceasefire with Turkey two days after their jailed leader asked them to lay down their arms.

Abdullah √Ėcalan urged fellow members of his outlawed Kurdistan Workers' Party to disarm and disband. The group, which has fought for autonomy since the 1980s, is considered a terrorist organization in the EU, US and Turkey. Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan welcomed √Ėcalan's overture, but warned against what he called provocations aiming to disrupt peace efforts.

items.title: More than 7,000 deaths in DRC since January | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The security situation in the east of Democratic Republic of Congo has reached "alarming levels" according to authorities. Over 7,000 people have been killed in the conflict since the start of the year. We speak to the UN's head of the peacekeeping mission MONUSCO. And we meet digital workers in Kenya facing trauma and stress from processing gruesome data for AI systems.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: PM Netanjahu is facing pressure from within his far-right coalition to continue the war | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The first six-week phase of the Gaza ceasefire expires. Hamas militants have released dozens of hostages in exchange for Israel's setting free thousands of Palestinians held at Israeli prisons. Under the terms of the agreement, fighting is to remain suspended as long as the two sides negotiate over a second stage. Those talks began in Cairo on Thursday but have so far been inconclusive. 

Chapters:
00:00 First stage of Israel-Hamas ceasefire set to expire
02:05 Hans-Jakob Schindler | Counter Extremism Project

items.title: Trump and Zelenskyy deal collapses after historic clash | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>What began as a standard press conference ahead of signing a mineral deal quickly turned into shouting and assertions from US President Donald Trump and his Vice President JD Vance that Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was being "disrespectful" and not "thankful," in addition to criticizing Zelenskyy's tremendous "hatred for Vladimir Putin." Zelenskyy left the White House soon after the meeting ended and no mineral deal was signed.

items.title: Can relations between the USA and Ukraine still be salvaged? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>European leaders have reinforced their support for Ukraine following a dramatic row between Donald Trump and Volodymyr Zelenskyy at the White House. French President Emmanuel Macron called Russia the aggressor in the war againt Ukraine - while EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas suggested the alliance between the US and Europe needed a new leader. 
Talks at the Oval Office descended into a fiery argument, with Trump accusing Zelenskyy of being disrepectful. It's thrown into doubt a planned minerals deal between the two countries - and US backing for Ukraine. 

Chapters:
00:00 Europeans back Ukraine after White House row
03:03 Stefan Simons, DW Reporter
08:38 Konstantin Eggert, DW Russia Analyst

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Vatican says Pope experienced bronchial spasm and difficulty breathing, yet currently 'alert'
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Vatican says Pope Francis, who is in hospital with pneumonia, has suffered a "breathing crisis." The statement said the pontiff was given air and responded well. People have been praying outside Rome's Gemelli Hospital, where the 88 year-old has spent the past two weeks. It's Pope Francis' longest hospital stay since he became pope in 2013.

#popefrancis #popesick
#vatican

items.title: Does Europe's top brass support for Zelenskyy signal a willingness to boost military aid to Ukraine?
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A dramatic row between Donald Trump and Volodymyr Zelenskyy has completely derailed Ukraine's efforts to secure a war-time deal with the United States. Talks at the Oval Office descended into a fiery argument, with President Trump accusing President Zelenskyy of being disrepectful and gambling with World War Three. A senior White House official says Trump now has no interest in the minerals deal that was meant to be signed in exchange for US military support. 

Chapter Breakdown:
0:00 Zelenskyy leaves White House after confrontation with President Trump and VP Vance
3:15 DW Correspondent Benjamin Alvarez Gruber in Washington on what led up to Trump's team telling Zelenskyy to leave the White House 
10:25 DW Correspondent Teri Schultz on European leaders' reactions to Trump-Zelenskyy spat
14:07 Angela Stent, Professor at Georgetown and former National Intelligence Officer for Russia and Eurasia at National Intelligence Council

items.title: First day of Germany's exploratory coalition talks end on 'constructive' note | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Germany's Christian Democratic Union (CDU) and Social Democratic Party (SPD) have called their first day of exploratory coalition talks 'open and constructive'. Friedrich Merz's conservative alliance won Sunday's elections and are hoping to form a coalition with the Social Demorats who came in third. The two sides are hoping to reach a swift agreement on issues such as defense spending and controlling irregular migration. Merz hopes to form a new government before the Easter holidays seven weeks from now. 

DW Correspondent Simon Young reports on first day of exploratory coalition talks in Germany. 

#germancoalition
 #cdu #trump
